rationale: Full re-review after PR #40 amended the DI text (a probe that can no longer execute is a per-probe gate failure, reported without aborting the replay) and strengthened the test with a typo'd-selector probe (must replay as an error, not a kill) and a vanished-file probe (reported failure while the remaining probe still runs). All probes via rdm story mutation-probe on rdm/story_audit/design_gate.py, all KILLED. Clauses: (1) probes stored as structured data: record_verdict '"probes": probes or [],' -> '"probes": [],' KILLED by the JSON round-trip equality; (2) replay re-executes the recorded killing probes: replay_probes 'killed += 1' -> 'pass' KILLED by (replayed, killed, failures) == (1, 1, []); (3) replay fails when a probe no longer kills: discarding the SURVIVES failure append (assign to _) KILLED by len(failures) == 1 and 'SURVIVES' in failures[0] after the test is weakened; (4) a probe run that errors is a reported gate failure, never a kill: discarding the error-branch failures.append (assign to _) KILLED (the typo'd-selector probe must produce killed == 0 AND one failure containing 'error'; the vanished-file probe one failure naming 'vanished.py'); (5) a probe that can no longer execute is a per-probe failure, not a crash of the replay: re-raising in the except OSError handler ('outcome = {"error": ...}' -> 'raise') KILLED (with the file gone the replay must not propagate, and the remaining healthy probe must still run: replayed == 2, killed == 1); (6) --stale filters the report to non-faithful inputs, both directions: 'if stale_only and agg.status == faithfulness.FAITHFUL:' -> 'if stale_only:' (hide everything) KILLED by 'DI-1: unreviewed' in the output, and -> 'if False:' (no filter) KILLED by 'DI-2' absent from the output. No uncovered clauses.

probes.find: failures.append(f"{di_id}: recorded killing probe now SURVIVES "
